title: Isla's New Friend
synopsis: Isla loves playing at the park but feels shy about making new friends. When she meets Maya, a girl who also loves the swings, Isla learns that friendship can bloom when you're brave enough to share and be kind.

pages.text: Isla loved going to the park with her mom. She had long wavy red hair that bounced when she ran. Her bright green eyes sparkled in the sunshine. The park was her favorite place in the whole world.

illustration.description: A sunny playground scene with Isla, who has long wavy red hair, very fair freckled skin, bright green eyes, walking into a colorful park with her mom. Bright primary colors show swings, slides, and other playground equipment with white space around them.
illustration.alt: Isla entering a bright sunny playground with her mother

pages.text: Every day, Isla would swing high on the swings. She would go down the big yellow slide. She would climb on the monkey bars. But Isla always played alone. She felt too shy to talk to the other kids.

illustration.description: Isla with long wavy red hair, very fair freckled skin, bright green eyes playing alone on bright primary colored playground equipment including swings and a yellow slide, with diverse kids playing in the background in a joyful energetic scene.
illustration.alt: Isla playing alone on playground equipment while other children play nearby

pages.text: Isla's mom sat on a bench and watched her play. Sometimes other moms would talk to Isla's mom. But Isla just kept playing by herself. She wished she could make a friend, but she didn't know how.

illustration.description: A park bench scene with Isla's mom talking to other parents while Isla with long wavy red hair, very fair freckled skin, bright green eyes plays nearby on the swings, surrounded by bright primary colors and white space with other diverse children laughing in the background.
illustration.alt: Isla's mother sitting on a park bench while Isla plays alone on the swings

pages.text: One sunny Tuesday, Isla saw a new girl at the park. The girl had dark curly hair and was wearing a bright blue dress. She was sitting alone on a swing, not moving. She looked sad.

illustration.description: A sunny playground scene showing Isla with long wavy red hair, very fair freckled skin, bright green eyes looking at a new girl with dark curly hair in a bright blue dress sitting alone on a swing, with bright primary colors and joyful playground elements in the background.
illustration.alt: Isla observing a new girl sitting alone on a swing at the playground

pages.text: Isla wanted to go talk to the girl, but her tummy felt funny. What if the girl didn't want to play? What if she said something wrong? Isla just watched from far away while she played on the slide.

illustration.description: Isla with long wavy red hair, very fair freckled skin, bright green eyes at the top of a bright primary colored slide, looking across the playground at the girl on the swing, with diverse kids playing around them in an energetic inclusive park setting with white space.
illustration.alt: Isla on a slide looking nervously at the new girl across the playground

pages.text: The new girl got up and walked to the monkey bars. She tried to swing across, but she couldn't reach the next bar. She tried again and again, but her arms weren't long enough. Isla could see she was getting upset.

illustration.description: The new girl with dark curly hair struggling to reach monkey bars while Isla with long wavy red hair, very fair freckled skin, bright green eyes watches from nearby, set in a bright sunny playground with primary colors and diverse children playing with laughter and friendship activities visible.
illustration.alt: The new girl struggling with monkey bars while Isla watches with concern

pages.text: Isla took a deep breath. She remembered what her mom always said: 'Be kind and brave, little one.' Isla walked over to the monkey bars. Her heart was beating fast, but she smiled at the girl.

illustration.description: Isla with long wavy red hair, very fair freckled skin, bright green eyes walking toward the monkey bars with a gentle smile, approaching the new girl in a bright sunny playground setting with primary colors, white space, and diverse kids playing joyfully in the background.
illustration.alt: Isla bravely walking toward the new girl at the monkey bars

pages.text: 'Hi,' said Isla softly. 'I'm Isla. The monkey bars are really hard. I couldn't do them when I first came here either.' The girl looked up and smiled a little. 'I'm Maya,' she said. 'This is my first time at this park.'

illustration.description: Isla with long wavy red hair, very fair freckled skin, bright green eyes talking to Maya near bright primary colored monkey bars, both girls smiling in a sunny playground with diverse children playing on swings and slides in an inclusive, energetic setting with white space.
illustration.alt: Isla and Maya having their first conversation by the monkey bars

pages.text: 'Would you like to swing with me?' asked Isla. 'Swinging is my favorite thing to do.' Maya's whole face lit up. 'I love swings too!' she said. 'But I was too scared to ask anyone to play with me.'

illustration.description: Isla with long wavy red hair, very fair freckled skin, bright green eyes and Maya standing together smiling brightly near the swings in a sunny playground filled with primary colors, diverse kids playing, and joyful energetic friendship activities with white space around them.
illustration.alt: Isla and Maya smiling together as they head toward the swings

pages.text: Isla and Maya ran to the swings together. They pumped their legs and swung higher and higher. They laughed as the wind blew through their hair. 'This is so much fun!' said Maya. 'It's even better with a friend!' said Isla.

illustration.description: Isla with long wavy red hair, very fair freckled skin, bright green eyes and Maya swinging high together on bright primary colored swings, both laughing joyfully in a sunny playground with diverse children playing around them and white space highlighting their friendship.
illustration.alt: Isla and Maya swinging together happily at the playground

pages.text: After swinging, they went down the slide together. They played on the seesaw. They even tried the monkey bars again, and Isla helped Maya reach the next bar. They played until their moms said it was time to go home.

illustration.description: Isla with long wavy red hair, very fair freckled skin, bright green eyes helping Maya at the monkey bars while diverse children play on bright primary colored playground equipment including slides and swings in a joyful energetic inclusive park setting with white space.
illustration.alt: Isla helping Maya with the monkey bars while other children play nearby

pages.text: 'Will you come back tomorrow?' asked Maya. 'Yes!' said Isla. 'We can play together every day!' They hugged goodbye. Isla felt so happy. She had been brave and made her very first friend at the park.

illustration.description: Isla with long wavy red hair, very fair freckled skin, bright green eyes and Maya hugging goodbye in a bright sunny playground with their mothers in the background, surrounded by primary colors, diverse children playing, and friendship activities with joyful energetic atmosphere and white space.
illustration.alt: Isla and Maya hugging goodbye while their mothers watch from nearby

metadata.illustrationStyle: bright sunny playground, diverse kids playing, primary colors, white space, joyful energetic, inclusive, park swings slides, laughter, friendship activities

questions.question: What was Isla's favorite thing to do at the park?
questions.explanation: Isla loved swinging on the swings - it was her favorite activity at the park!

questions.question: Why was Maya sitting alone on the swing when Isla first saw her?
choices: She was sad and it was her first time at the park
She was waiting for her mom
questions.explanation: Maya was new to the park and felt too scared to ask anyone to play with her, just like Isla felt shy too.

questions.question: What did Isla's mom always tell her?
choices: Be kind and brave, little one
questions.explanation: Isla remembered her mom's words 'Be kind and brave, little one' when she decided to talk to Maya.

questions.question: What playground equipment did Maya have trouble with?
questions.explanation: Maya couldn't reach the next bar on the monkey bars because her arms weren't long enough.

questions.question: How did Isla help Maya at the end of the story?
choices: She helped Maya reach the monkey bars
She pushed Maya on the swing
She went down the slide with her
questions.explanation: Isla helped Maya reach the next bar on the monkey bars, showing how friends help each other.
